tldr: Move from Keep's open-source AIOps alert aggregation to Anteon's Kubernetes-focused observability platform.
why_switch: Anteon offers a hosted option, reducing self-host ops burden.
Integrated logs/metrics/traces in one platform vs alert-only Keep.
K8s eBPF agent provides deep cluster visibility.
Useful if your need has shifted from alert correlation to full-stack observability.
what_to_export: List of connected alert sources and their credentials
Audit history and recent alert payloads worth keeping
export_method: Keep stores configuration in its database and exposes APIs/UI for rule management. Use the Keep API or back up the underlying database to pull rules, workflows, and integration configs. Refer to keephq.dev documentation for the current export approach.
import_method: Anteon installs as a hosted SaaS or self-hosted (open-core) deployment with K8s agents. Provision a workspace, install the agent into each cluster, and recreate alert/correlation logic inside Anteon's rules engine. Reconnect notification channels and integrations. Consult Anteon's official docs for exact install and configuration steps.

concept_mapping.note: Recreate connections to upstream tools per Anteon's supported integrations list.
Confirm exact equivalents in Anteon docs; semantics differ.
Anteon is observability-centric; AIOps depth may not match Keep.
Workflow automation may need an external tool (n8n, etc.).
Choose deployment model based on data residency needs.
Anteon billing is usage-based — model your data volumes first.

gotchas: Anteon is not primarily an AIOps alert manager — feature parity for correlation is unlikely.
Pricing is usage-based; estimate ingest before committing.
If Keep's 50+ provider catalog matters, verify each integration exists in Anteon.
Self-host Keep config won't import; rules must be re-authored.
Plan a dual-run period to validate alert noise and routing.
AGPL-3.0 self-host option for Anteon — review license if you customize.

email.subject: Migrating from Keep to Anteon
email.body:
Hi team,

Considering a move from Keep to Anteon. The driver is consolidating logs, metrics, traces, and alerts into one platform with a hosted option, instead of running Keep alongside our other observability stack.

Caveat: Anteon is observability-first, not an AIOps alert correlator. We'll need to verify that key Keep features (deduplication, correlation, workflows) have acceptable equivalents — or keep Keep for that layer.

Work: export Keep rules and integrations; provision Anteon; install agents; re-author alerts; reconnect Slack/PagerDuty; run in parallel before cutover.

Estimated effort: 8-24 hours, plus parallel-run.

Will follow up with a comparison doc and pricing estimate.

Thanks.
